author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: 2020 Block Island Arts & Artisans Festival at the Narragansett Inn
summary: This island tradition takes place once a year on the Harbor Church lawn right in town. This three day festival is brimming with all things artistic; oil or water color paintings, jewelry, pottery, jewelry, fabrics, candles,photographs and other hand made pieces created by a very talented group of artists.
content: This island tradition takes place once a year on the Harbor Church lawn right in town. This three day festival is brimming with all things artistic; oil or water color paintings, jewelry, pottery, jewelry, fabrics, candles,photographs and other hand made pieces created by a very talented group of artists.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Annual House and Garden Tour
summary: Annual Block Island Historical Society House and Garden Tour
content:
Annual Block Island Historical Society House and Garden Tour

Historic cottages along Connecticut Avenue, Center Road & off Corn Neck Road.

Tickets available at: The BI Historical Museum, the Island Bound Book Store, the Glass Onion, and online at blockislandhistorical.org. 

For more information contact us at (401) 466-2481 or  blockislandhistory@gmail.com.

Adults: $40 | Members: $35

Sponsored by: e Sea Breeze Inn, Block Island Grocery, Ballard’s, Yellow Kittens, Aldo’s Restaurant and Block Island Realty, Glass Onion, Bethany’s Diner, Hotel Manisses, National Hotel e Museum Island Bound Book Store e Glass Onion online at blockislandhistorical.org

meta.description: Annual Block Island House and Garden Tour

author: Will Gasner
title: Palatine Gravesite Tour
summary: Tour with Executive Director Pam Gasner & Jill Farinelli Author of The Palatine Wreck
content:
Tour with Executive Director Pam Gasner & Jill Farinelli Author of The Palatine Wreck

Meet at the Block Island Historical Society @ 3:45

Tickets: $30 per person, $25 per Member of the Museum

Public reception to follow at Island Bound Bookstore for book-signing by author Jill Farinelli!

author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Maritime Institute Harbor Tour
summary: Harbor Tour of the Great Salt Pond with BI Maritime Institute (BIMI).
content:
Harbor Tour of the Great Salt Pond with BI Maritime Institute (BIMI).

Tours are every Monday from 10am to 11am.

meta.title: BI Maritime Institute Harbor Tour
meta.description: Learn about the Great Salt Pond on Block Island on this harbor tour from the BI Maritime Institute.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Country Fest at Ballard’s
summary: It’s a little bit of Nashville on Block Island at Ballard’s on Thursday, July 5. Grab your ten-gallon hat and your snakeskin cowboy boots (or your golf visor and Sperry’s) and head over to Ballard’s on Water St. for a toe-tapping’ foot-stompin’, finger-snappin’ afternoon of all-American music, starting at 10 am.
content:
It’s a little bit of Nashville on Block Island at Ballard’s on Thursday, July 5. Grab your ten-gallon hat and your snakeskin cowboy boots (or your golf visor and Sperry’s) and head over to Ballard’s on Water St. for a toe-tapping’ foot-stompin’, finger-snappin’ afternoon of all-American music, starting at 10 am.

Featured country artists to be announced for 2018. 

If your taste runs a little more to the country-folk-rock spectrum, stick around for the evening as house favorite John Brazile, a singer-songwriter hailing from Worcester, MA, brings his soulful sound to Ballard’s starting around 6 p.m. John’s been a regular at Ballard’s for years now. He loves the Island and the Island loves him. Come on out to Ballard’s and find out why.

meta.title: Country Fest at Ballard’s
meta.description: The Country Fest at Ballard’s will be on the 5th of July 2018.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Sound Baths at Spring Street Gallery
summary: Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.
content:
Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.   

All you have to do is relax and listen.

Yoga mats, blankets, pillows and silk eye covers will be provided.

Anyone is welcome to bring their own yoga mat or chair (or beanbag chair if you’re amazing).

Space is limited;  first come, first serve.

 

$25 for a 45 min sound bath. cash, check or venmo accepted

author: Austin Crawford
title: Nature Walk & Community Science Juvenile Horseshoe Crab Survey
summary: Meet at Andy's Way parking area
content:
Meet at Andy's Way parking area 
Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats.

cherring@tnc.org

401-932-2941
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Film Festival - “Coffee Time” at Champlin's
summary: Coffee Time with the Block Island Film Festival hosts, actor/producers Kristin Carey and Henry LeBlanc, who will talk about their journey in Hollywood and the 2023 film festival. Check out the BIFF 2023 schedule: https://blockislandfilmfestival.org/schedule. Film Festival is May 31 - June 2, 2023. Meet at the Cafe Boutique at 10:30am.
content: Coffee Time with the Block Island Film Festival hosts, actor/producers Kristin Carey and Henry LeBlanc, who will talk about their journey in Hollywood and the 2023 film festival. Check out the BIFF 2023 schedule: https://blockislandfilmfestival.org/schedule. Film Festival is May 31 - June 2, 2023. Meet at the Cafe Boutique at 10:30am.

title: Canine pet first aid and CPR training postponed
